patient_presentation.presenting_complaint: Six weeks of polyarthralgia, fatigue, and a photosensitive facial rash
patient_presentation.history: 28-year-old female, no significant PMH. On combined oral contraceptive for 2 years. Mother has rheumatoid arthritis. Reports symmetric MCP/PIP/wrist pain with morning stiffness ~45 min, intermittent low-grade fevers, and a malar rash that worsens after sun exposure. No recent travel, no sick contacts, no new medications.

imaging.notes: Hand XR: no erosions, mild soft tissue swelling. CXR: clear.

Hauser.reasoning: Carmen's about to scream lupus. Forman's about to mumble viral. The interesting question nobody's asking: is this Mixed Connective Tissue Disease? Speckled ANA at high titer is the MCTD fingerprint, and her symptoms could be the early myositis/arthritis overlap before it declares itself.

differentials.rationale: Speckled ANA 1:640 is classically associated with anti-U1-RNP and MCTD. Overlap features (arthritis, fatigue, low-grade fever) without yet a single defining diagnosis fit early MCTD.
Meets several EULAR/ACR criteria already.
OCPs can unmask or trigger lupus but rarely cause classic DIL.
contradicting_evidence: DIL usually anti-histone, low complements unusual

proposed_tests.rationale: Differentiate MCTD vs SLE vs Sjögren overlap

challenges.content: Carmen, don't lock in SLE before you've ruled out MCTD. Speckled pattern at 1:640 is suspicious for U1-RNP.

Forman.reasoning: Base rates matter. A 28F with 6 weeks of arthralgia, fatigue, and rash: parvovirus B19, EBV, and post-viral arthropathy are far more common than SLE. Yes, ANA is positive, but ANA is positive in 5-15% of healthy young women. I want to anchor this in evidence before committing to lifelong immunosuppression.

differentials.rationale: Common in young women, can cause symmetric small joint arthritis, rash, transient cytopenias.
contradicting_evidence: Photosensitive distribution of rash atypical for parvo

differentials.rationale: Meets criteria but I want anti-dsDNA confirmation before committing.
Mother has RA, MCP/PIP involvement.

challenges.content: Carmen, anti-dsDNA is pending. Don't anchor on SLE on the basis of an ANA and low complements alone — the differential here is wide and viral arthropathy is more prevalent.

Carmen.reasoning: This is SLE until proven otherwise. She already has malar rash, photosensitivity, arthralgia, leukopenia, ANA 1:640, and hypocomplementemia — that's 5 EULAR/ACR domains positive. The pending anti-dsDNA is a confirmatory step, not a gating one. Forman's viral hypothesis doesn't explain the low complements.

differentials.rationale: Multiple ACR/EULAR criteria met. Hypocomplementemia is doing heavy lifting — viral syndromes don't consume C3/C4 like this.
contradicting_evidence: Speckled (not homogeneous) ANA pattern slightly atypical

differentials.rationale: Hauser's point is fair. Speckled pattern fits, but full MCTD usually needs Raynaud's and sclerodactyly.
Possible early-stage label if criteria stay short.
supporting_evidence: Multi-system but not all criteria met
contradicting_evidence: She's likely going to declare as SLE

proposed_tests.rationale: Screen for lupus nephritis given trace protein

challenges.content: Forman, parvo doesn't drop complements. You're letting base rate drag you past the actual data on the table.

Chen.reasoning: No surgical or ICU pathology. No serositis with tamponade physiology, no acute abdomen, no thrombosis. I have nothing useful to add yet — happy to be called back if she develops a procedural need (e.g., renal biopsy).

differentials.rationale: Following team consensus.

Wills.reasoning: Young woman, B-symptoms (fatigue, low-grade fevers, weight loss not yet quantified), cytopenias. Lymphoma can mimic autoimmune disease. I want to make sure we're not missing it before we commit to immunosuppression. That said, the serologic profile screams autoimmune, not malignancy.

differentials.rationale: B-symptoms + cytopenias warrant consideration but serology argues against.
contradicting_evidence: ANA 1:640 with low complements is not a lymphoma signature

differentials.rationale: Concur with rheumatology lean.

proposed_tests.test_name: LDH, peripheral smear, exam for lymphadenopathy/HSM

Carmen.reasoning: Forman wants base rates; I'll give him a Bayesian update. Pretest for SLE in this presentation is high. The single test that splits this cleanly is anti-dsDNA + repeat complements with a urine protein:creatinine. I'll order them and we revisit.

differentials.rationale: Holding probability pending dsDNA. Even if dsDNA negative, clinical + complement criteria carry weight.
Will be addressed by ENA panel.
Acknowledging Forman's point but it doesn't explain hypocomplementemia.

proposed_tests.rationale: Highly specific for SLE; titer correlates with activity

challenges.content: If dsDNA comes back positive, we are done debating. If negative, I'll meet you halfway on UCTD.

Forman.reasoning: Fair. The complement consumption is the part of the data my viral hypothesis doesn't fit. I'll wait for the dsDNA, but I'm raising my SLE prior. I still want parvo serology back to close the loop, and RF/CCP given the family history.

differentials.rationale: Updated upward; complement pattern is the deciding piece.
Lower than round 1 — does not explain complements.
Pending serology

scripted_rounds.new_evidence: Anti-dsDNA returns positive at 1:160. Repeat C3 55, C4 9 (still consumed). Parvovirus B19 IgM negative, IgG positive (remote). RF negative, anti-CCP negative. Urine protein:creatinine 0.6 (sub-nephrotic proteinuria).

Carmen.reasoning: Anti-dsDNA 1:160 plus persistent hypocomplementemia plus sub-nephrotic proteinuria — this is SLE with likely early lupus nephritis. We need a renal biopsy to grade the nephritis class before we choose induction therapy.

differentials.rationale: Anti-dsDNA positive, low complements, proteinuria — meets SLE classification with renal involvement.
supporting_evidence: Malar rash, photosensitivity, leukopenia, arthritis, ANA 1:640

differentials.rationale: Less likely now that dsDNA is positive.
Effectively excluded.

proposed_tests.rationale: Grade lupus nephritis (Class I-VI) to guide induction therapy
Standard SLE workup, affects pregnancy/thrombosis risk on OCP

Forman.reasoning: I concede. Anti-dsDNA positive with low complements and proteinuria is SLE. I'll co-sign the renal biopsy plan and start working on her medication reconciliation — OCP needs to come off given APLS risk and SLE flare risk with estrogen.

differentials.rationale: Updated based on dsDNA result.
Excluded.

proposed_tests.test_name: Discontinue OCP, switch to progestin-only or non-hormonal
proposed_tests.rationale: Estrogen worsens SLE and APLS thrombotic risk

Carmen.reasoning: Renal biopsy shows Class III focal proliferative lupus nephritis. Initiating hydroxychloroquine + induction with mycophenolate mofetil and tapering steroids. SLE confirmed with renal involvement. Team consensus reached.

differentials.diagnosis: SLE with Class III lupus nephritis
differentials.rationale: Biopsy-confirmed.

proposed_tests.rationale: Standard of care for Class III LN

Forman.reasoning: Concur. SLE with Class III LN. Treatment plan is appropriate. Will follow renal function, complements, dsDNA titers as activity markers.

differentials.diagnosis: SLE with Class III lupus nephritis
differentials.rationale: Biopsy confirmation closes the case.

Hauser.reasoning: Fine. It was SLE, not MCTD. The U1-RNP came back negative anyway. I keep my pride in the fact that I made you look at the speckled pattern instead of just stamping 'lupus' on the chart.

differentials.diagnosis: SLE with Class III lupus nephritis
differentials.rationale: Conceded to team consensus given biopsy.
Excluded by negative U1-RNP and biopsy.
